name: Lanford Wood
overview: Landford Wood (also written Landfordwood, and recorded here as Lanford Wood) is a small, scattered hamlet in the civil parish of Landford in south-east Wiltshire, within the New Forest National Park, about 10 miles south-east of Salisbury and immediately north of Landford village. It lies in former Royal Forest of Melchet woodland near the Wiltshire-Hampshire border, and is one of the parish's named hamlets alongside Hamptworth and Nomansland.
character: A very small, wooded forest-edge settlement of cottages and houses dispersed among trees along quiet lanes.
amenities: The New Forest CE Primary School at Landford/Nomansland
Village shop, post office and the Landford Poacher pub at nearby Landford
No facilities within the hamlet itself

schools.name: The New Forest Church of England Primary School

commutes.notes: About 10 miles north-west, partly via the A36, for shops, services and the station
Via the A36 to the south-east
From Salisbury on South Western Railway; fastest services about 1h 20m

prices.summary: A very small stock of cottages and detached houses in a wooded New Forest setting, with low transaction volumes; the National Park location supports premium values. See the live Rightmove sold-price page for current figures.

thingsToDo: Walking and cycling in the New Forest National Park
Exploring the nearby Landford Bog and Landford Heath Sites of Special Scientific Interest
Woodland and common walks in the former Melchet forest
pros: Quiet, wooded New Forest National Park setting
Close to Landford's shop, pub and primary school
Easy access to the A36 for Salisbury and Southampton
cons: No amenities within the hamlet itself
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a tiny, secluded woodland hamlet on the New Forest edge with Landford's everyday amenities close by.

sources.label: Ofsted - The New Forest CE Primary School (URN 148024, Good, Dec 2024)
